affordances.name: Set context with merit and consent check
affordances.mechanism: When the frame before an ask will shape interpretation, use pre-suasion to make the core message legible and fairly evaluated, while checking that the attention cue is relevant to the proposal's merits and not covert manipulation.

use_when: Attention placement will shape how the next message is interpreted.
The audience is overloaded, distracted, or relying on default associations.
A complex idea needs to become legible before it is defended in detail.
do_not_use_when: The context cue is decoupled from the merits of the proposal.
The technique would steer the audience without transparency or rational engagement.
The model would prime a desired conclusion in place of evidence testing.
case_evidence_needed: The preliminary attention cue or frame.
Why that cue is substantively relevant to the merits.
The rational proof or detail that remains available after the initial frame.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to use a preliminary frame only when it helps the audience fairly understand the substance and preserves later deliberation.
evidence_required: The substantive link between the frame and proposal.
The proof or caveat that should follow the frame.
good_output_shape: Checks that the frame is relevant to the merits.
Keeps rational proof and consent visible.

treatment_requirements.description: Require the answer to identify any incidental mood, setting, or associative cue that may be biasing judgment, test whether it is relevant to the merits, and restore factual proof before treating the judgment as reliable.
evidence_required: The incidental cue, mood, or setting that may be shaping interpretation.
The decision, ask, or judgment being influenced.
Whether the cue is relevant to the merits or only associative atmosphere.
The factual proof available after the irrelevant association is corrected.
good_output_shape: Corrects irrelevant association before relying on judgment.
Preserves factual proof and later deliberation.

diagnostic_questions: What frame will the audience bring before the ask arrives?
Is the context cue relevant to the merits or just persuasive atmosphere?
What proof will the audience get after the emotional or associative frame?
misuse_guards: Do not use pre-suasion as covert control.
Do not let priming replace evidence testing.
Do not use an attention cue you would be ashamed to explain.

source_evidence.source_quote: attention placement will shape how the next message is interpreted
decoupled from the actual merits of the proposal

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 15 outcome: strong_affordance_record. The source supports pre-suasion only as context-setting with merit, proof, and ethics checks.
dropped_material: Did not extract manipulative tricks as recommended tactics.
Did not create persuasive product copy.
open_questions: Later packet review should test whether the card helps the LLM reject manipulation while preserving legitimate sequencing.

absence_records.reason: The source explicitly names ethical risk and manipulation, so covert control is not a supported operational use.
The source's mitigation asks whether the context is relevant to the merits and explainable, so arbitrary context cues are not supported.

source_evidence.source_quote: Is the context I am setting truly relevant to the merits of the proposal

absence_records.reason: The source allows emotional framing only when rational and technical proof remains available. Do not promote a pre-suasive emotional sale that withholds the factual basis needed for later deliberation.

The source's good-mood example shows that irrelevant primes should be corrected, not exploited. Do not use mood, setting, or associative atmosphere as leverage when it is unrelated to the decision merits.
The source explicitly warns that pre-suasion can prime a desired conclusion and undermine rigorous hypothesis testing. Do not promote context-setting when it replaces open-minded evidence testing.

source_evidence.source_quote: priming a favorable associative pattern leads to motivated reasoning

absence_records.reason: The source describes simulated trust as a brilliant but ethically risky trick. Do not promote trust theater before an ask; route real costly value, receptivity, or proof to reciprocity, liking, or signaling owner records.

source_evidence.source_quote: make a complex idea legible before it is defended in detail

review_notes.normalization_note: Batch 15 source reading found strong reviewed depth for pre-suasion as ethical sequencing with merit checks. PR80 hardens incidental-prime correction as a treatment requirement inside the existing context-setting card. PR96 adds rails against emotional sale without proof, irrelevant primes, confirmation-biased context setting, and trust theater.
dropped_material: No runtime, prompt, lane, packet, deterministic pressure, or user-facing change is implied.
open_questions: A later packet should test whether this card helps separate framing from manipulation.
